title: carex pilulifera subsp. azorica
intro: Carex pilulifera subsp. azorica, also known as Azores-White Sedge, is a subspecies of perennial sedge native to the Azores, a group of volcanic islands in the North Atlantic Ocean. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Carex pilulifera subsp. azorica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
